title: Fix Orbit Dock interaction: swipe-only active state, tap-to-open, side-icon rotation-first behavior

requirements.summary: Remove auto-open on swipe: swiping updates only visual active state without triggering navigation
acceptanceCriteria: Swiping the dock left or right moves items along the arc and updates which item is visually active (centered).
After a swipe completes, no view, dialog, or panel opens automatically.
The existing infinite circular swipe, scale hierarchy, opacity hierarchy, and orbital animation remain unchanged.

file_operations.description: Remove any auto-open logic triggered by swipe gesture completion or currentIndex changes. Ensure swipe handlers (onSwipeLeft, onSwipeRight) only update the currentIndex state via setCurrentIndex, without invoking any of the open/navigation callbacks (onUploadOpen, onFoldersOpen, onMissionsOpen). Preserve all existing visual behaviors: infinite circular positioning, scale hierarchy, opacity hierarchy, and orbital animation. Review the component to identify where swipe completion might trigger opens (e.g., useEffect watching currentIndex, or direct callback invocations in swipe handlers) and remove those triggers.

requirements.summary: Implement open-on-tap logic: centered icon opens only when tapped while already in center position
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping the centered (active) icon opens the corresponding function (Upload, Folders, or Mission).
All existing open/navigation callbacks for each function continue to work correctly once triggered by this tap.
No other interaction (swipe, tap on side icon) opens a function.

file_operations.description: Add onClick handlers to each dock icon that check if the tapped item's index matches currentIndex. If the tapped item is the centered (active) item, invoke the corresponding open callback (onUploadOpen for Upload, onFoldersOpen for Folders, onMissionsOpen for Mission). If the tapped item is not centered, do not invoke any open callback (rotation-first behavior will be handled in REQ-3). Ensure the tap logic distinguishes between center taps (which open) and side taps (which rotate).

requirements.summary: Implement rotation-first for side icon taps: tapping inactive icon rotates it to center without opening; second tap opens
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping a side icon triggers a smooth rotation animation that brings it to the center position.
After the rotation, only the visual active state changes — the function does not open.
A subsequent tap on the now-centered icon opens the function.
The rotation animation reuses the existing orbital/smooth-rotation mechanism without introducing new animation systems.

file_operations.description: Extend the onClick handlers added in REQ-2 to implement rotation-first behavior for side (non-centered) icon taps. When a side icon is tapped (tapped index !== currentIndex), call setCurrentIndex to the tapped item's index, triggering the existing smooth rotation animation provided by useCircularCarousel. Do not invoke any open callback during this rotation. The existing carousel transition logic will animate the rotation. After rotation completes and the item becomes centered, a second tap will trigger the open logic from REQ-2. Ensure the rotation uses the existing CSS transitions and does not introduce new animation systems.

requirements.summary: Add subtle tap feedback: scale-down animation and light haptic vibration on all icon taps
acceptanceCriteria: Every tap on any dock icon (center or side) produces a brief scale-down to 0.95 lasting 100–120ms, then returns to the normal scale.
On devices/browsers that support navigator.vibrate, a short light vibration (≤15ms) fires on each tap.
On devices that do not support haptics, the tap interaction proceeds silently without errors.
The scale feedback does not conflict with the existing scale/opacity hierarchy applied by the carousel positioning.

file_operations.description: Add visual and haptic feedback to all dock icon onClick handlers. On every tap (whether center or side icon), apply a temporary scale transform to 0.95 for 100–120ms using inline style or a CSS class, then remove it to return to the carousel-computed scale. Use CSS transition for the scale animation. Additionally, invoke navigator.vibrate([10]) (or a duration ≤15ms) on each tap if the API is supported; wrap the vibrate call in a try-catch or feature-detection check to avoid errors on unsupported devices. Ensure the temporary scale does not interfere with the existing scale hierarchy from useCircularCarousel by applying it additively or using a separate CSS property/class that animates independently.
